name: Signal Waveform
one_liner: A glowing phosphor oscilloscope: stacked broadcast signal traces sweep left-to-right with afterglow decay, a gold graticule grid behind them, and a synced playhead -- the audio/video essence rendered as live scope lines.
visual_description: A near-black scope screen carries a faint gold graticule (the classic vectorscope/waveform-monitor grid -- IRE-style horizontal divisions and a center crosshair). Across it, three stacked glowing traces sweep continuously left-to-right: a phosphor-green primary waveform (the audio essence) that wobbles and beats with harmonic detail, a dimmer cyan trace above it (the video luma), and a thin magenta trace below (chroma). Each trace has a bright white-hot leading edge -- the sweep playhead -- with a long phosphor afterglow tail decaying behind it, exactly like a real CRT scope. The whole thing breathes on a steady PTP-style beat: every couple of seconds the traces flash brighter and a thin vertical sync bar wipes across. Heavy bloom blooms the white playhead and trace cores into green/cyan/magenta halos. A faint scanline + grain overlay and an occasional micro RGB-split at the brightest peak sell the broadcast-engineering authenticity. It reads instantly as 'a signal on a scope' -- the most literal video-engineering motif on the floor.
technique: Single self-contained GLSL TOP generator (no input), plus a Feedback TOP for the phosphor afterglow trail. The shader computes, per pixel, the distance to N analytic signal traces (summed detuned sines = a rich broadcast-like waveform), draws a thin bright line with a soft glow, layers a gold graticule grid, a sweeping white playhead with exponential afterglow, and a PTP sync flash. The afterglow is doubled by a light Feedback TOP (decay < 1, bounded) so the playhead leaves a real persistence trail like a CRT. Cheap per-pixel math; the only stateful element is the bounded feedback. Finished with bloom + scanline/grain in the same or a follow-on pass.

network_plan.name: glsl_scope
network_plan.role: The whole scope. Generator (no input): graticule + N analytic traces + sweeping playhead + PTP flash. Generator MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res (res=(1/w,1/h,w,h)); NEVER uTD2DInfos on a no-input generator. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float for HDR glow. Three vec4 uniforms via the vec SEQUENCE (declared manually in this glslTOP). Place nodeX 400, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: level_decay
network_plan.role: Decay stage for the phosphor afterglow feedback loop. opacity = Afterglow param (< 1) so the previous frame's trace fades. A small blacklevel keeps it from settling on noise. This bounds the feedback. Place nodeX 800, nodeY 200.

network_plan.name: comp_trail
network_plan.role: Combine the fresh scope frame with the decayed previous frame (additive max-ish) so the playhead leaves a CRT persistence tail. Input0 = glsl_scope (fresh), Input1 = level_decay (decayed history). operand = max (or add) so old trace glow persists but never runs away. Place nodeX 1100, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: feedback_glow
network_plan.role: Holds the previous combined frame for the afterglow. top = comp_trail (closes the loop). Fixed 1280x720 rgba16float so it never grows. The decay in level_decay (opacity < 1) keeps it bounded. Place nodeX 800, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_finish
network_plan.role: Bloom + broadcast finish. Input0 = comp_trail. Bright-pass + bounded blur bloom added back; faint scanline overlay; film grain; an occasional micro RGB-split at the brightest peaks on the PTP tick. outputresolution custom 1280x720, rgba16float. Place nodeX 1500, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: out1
network_plan.role: Manifest output_op / capture target, named exactly out1. Viewer flagged. Place nodeX 1900, nodeY 0.

network_plan.name: glsl_scope_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_scope. See glsl[0]. extension frag. Hug under host.

network_plan.name: glsl_scope_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_scope.

network_plan.name: glsl_finish_pixel
network_plan.role: Docked pixel shader for glsl_finish. See glsl[1]. extension frag.

network_plan.name: glsl_finish_compute
network_plan.role: Docked empty compute companion for glsl_finish.

network_plan.name: annotate_scope
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the scope generator (glsl_scope). Mode annotate, Opacity 0.3.

params.Bodytext: One generator GLSL TOP draws everything: a gold graticule grid, then per pixel the distance to N analytic signal traces (summed detuned sines = a rich broadcast waveform), a thin bright line with soft glow, and a sweeping white-hot playhead. Green = audio essence, cyan = video luma, magenta = chroma -- the ST2110 separated streams, on a scope.

network_plan.name: annotate_glow
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over the phosphor feedback loop (level_decay, comp_trail, feedback_glow). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: A bounded Feedback TOP gives the playhead a real CRT persistence tail: comp_trail maxes the fresh frame with the decayed previous frame, feedback_glow holds it (Target = comp_trail), and level_decay (opacity < 1) fades old trace energy so it can never run away.

network_plan.name: annotate_finish
network_plan.role: Teaching annotation over finish + out (glsl_finish, out1). Mode annotate.

params.Bodytext: Bloom blows the white playhead and trace cores into colored halos; a faint scanline + grain overlay and an occasional micro RGB-split on the PTP beat sell the broadcast-engineering authenticity. Terminates in out1.

exposed_params.name: Traces
exposed_params.label: Trace Count
exposed_params.drives: uWave.x -- number of stacked signal traces (1=green audio only, 2=+cyan video, 3=+magenta chroma). The ST2110 separated essences as scope lines.

exposed_params.name: Frequency
exposed_params.label: Frequency
exposed_params.drives: uWave.y -- base spatial frequency of the waveform. Low = slow rolling signal; high = dense rapid oscillation packed across the screen.

exposed_params.name: Amplitude
exposed_params.label: Amplitude
exposed_params.drives: uWave.z -- vertical swing of the traces. 0 = flat baselines; higher = tall dramatic waveforms (clamped so they stay on screen).

exposed_params.name: Speed
exposed_params.label: Signal Speed
exposed_params.drives: Multiplies absTime into uWave.w -- how fast the waveform content evolves (the signal changing over time). 0 freezes the waveform shape (the sweep still moves).

exposed_params.name: Sweeprate
exposed_params.label: Sweep Rate
exposed_params.drives: uSweep.x -- how fast the white-hot playhead sweeps left-to-right across the screen (Hz). The scope refresh feel.

exposed_params.name: Afterglow
exposed_params.label: Afterglow
exposed_params.drives: uSweep.y (in-shader persistence falloff) AND level_decay.opacity (the Feedback TOP decay). Higher = longer CRT phosphor persistence tail behind the playhead. Below 1 keeps the feedback bounded.

exposed_params.name: Linewidth
exposed_params.label: Line Width
exposed_params.drives: uSweep.w -- thickness of the trace core. Thin = a crisp precise scope line; thicker = a heavier glowing beam.

exposed_params.name: Graticule
exposed_params.label: Graticule
exposed_params.drives: uLook.x -- brightness of the gold graticule grid + center crosshair behind the traces. 0 hides the grid; higher makes the engineering scope frame prominent.

exposed_params.name: Harmonics
exposed_params.label: Harmonics
exposed_params.drives: uLook.y -- amount of high harmonic detail summed into the signal. 0 = a clean fundamental sine; higher = a rich, complex broadcast-like waveform.

exposed_params.name: Glow
exposed_params.label: Trace Glow
exposed_params.drives: uLook.z -- width of the soft halo around each trace before bloom. The signature scope-beam glow.

exposed_params.name: Ptprate
exposed_params.label: PTP Beat Rate
exposed_params.drives: uSweep.z (scope flash + sync bar) and uFinish.w (RGB-split event timing) -- frequency of the PTP sync pulse the whole scope breathes to.

exposed_params.name: Bloom
exposed_params.label: Bloom
exposed_params.drives: uFinish.x -- intensity of the bloom added back over the traces. The booth-scale glow knob.

exposed_params.name: Rgbsplit
exposed_params.label: RGB Split
exposed_params.drives: uFinish.z -- strength of the chromatic RGB separation event pulsed on the PTP beat. 0 = clean; higher = a pronounced broadcast chroma tear at each tick.

palette.ratio: ~85% dark scope ground + gold graticule restraint, ~15% saturated phosphor traces; green leads (the engineering signature), cyan/magenta differentiate the essences.

conference_fit: The single most legible 'video engineering' motif on the floor -- a phosphor-green oscilloscope trace reads as broadcast/signal louder than anything. On-brand: gold graticule + green trace is the literal vectorscope/waveform-monitor palette every broadcast engineer recognizes, on a dark premium stage. The PTP sync breath ties it to ST2110 timing; the three stacked traces (green/cyan/magenta) show the separated essences. Self-contained, cheap, and unmistakably on-theme; a perfect quieter counterpoint to the dense packet pieces.

prompt.one_liner: A glowing phosphor oscilloscope generator: stacked broadcast traces (green audio / cyan video / magenta chroma) sweeping left-to-right with a bounded-feedback CRT afterglow, a gold graticule, a PTP sync breath, and a bloom + scanline + RGB-split broadcast finish.
prompt.evolve: Raise Harmonics + Frequency for a busier signal; raise Afterglow for long phosphor smears; drop Speed to 0 to freeze the waveform while the sweep keeps running. Lower Trace Count to 1 for a pure audio scope. Sweep RGB Split + PTP Beat for stronger broadcast tears. Feed out1 into the kaleidoscope specimen, or drive Amplitude/Frequency from a real Audio File In + Analyze CHOP into the uniforms for a genuine reactive scope. Replace the analytic signal() with a sampled CHOP-to-texture for a true input-driven waveform monitor.

build_notes:
GENERATOR CORRECTNESS: glsl_scope has NO input -- it MUST use uTDOutputInfo.res (res=(1/w,1/h,w,h); .zw = pixel w,h) for resolution/aspect; using uTD2DInfos[0] on a no-input generator is a compile error. outputresolution MUST be 'custom' + resolutionw/h or it renders 1x1. glsl_finish HAS an input so sTD2DInputs[0] + uTDOutputInfo.res are both valid there.

UNIFORMS: both glslTOPs declare their uniforms MANUALLY (a glslTOP auto-declares nothing -- unlike a glslPOP). Set via the vec parameter SEQUENCE: op.seq.vec.numBlocks=N, vecNname, vecNtype='vec4', vecNvaluex..w as expressions. NOT op.par.vec (whose .eval() reads 0 and misleads).

FEEDBACK SAFETY (performance.md): the afterglow loop is bounded -- fixed 1280x720 rgba16float on BOTH comp_trail-feeding feedback_glow and the loop, level_decay opacity = Afterglow (<1, default 0.85), comp_trail uses operand=max so glow persists but cannot exceed the fresh frame's peak. While wiring, BYPASS feedback_glow until the chain is complete. Watch feedback_glow gpuCookTime / gpuMemUsedMB across checks; if either climbs every frame with no param change the decay went >=1. 16f not 32f -- this is a light persistence trail, not a precision sim.

WIRING ORDER (the loop is a cycle): create glsl_scope, level_decay, comp_trail, feedback_glow, glsl_finish, out1; wire glsl_scope->comp_trail(in0), level_decay->comp_trail(in1); comp_trail->feedback_glow; feedback_glow->level_decay; comp_trail->glsl_finish->out1. THEN set feedback_glow.top='comp_trail' (the Target closes the loop, not a wire).

COOK DEMAND: time-dependent through absTime in the uniforms and the feedback. Cooks every frame only when out1 is demanded. In the sandbox add a temp Execute DAT OUTSIDE the COMP (onFrameStart cooks out1); verify animation with two captures separated by a real sleep. Speed=0 freezes the waveform shape but the sweep/PTP still reference absTime so the TOP keeps cooking (a moving playhead over a frozen signal) -- correct, not a bug.

DOCKED DATS: glsl_scope and glsl_finish each dock pixel+compute+info; HUG all six under their hosts in the SAME execute_python that creates each op, then get_network_layout. Keep shader source LF/space-indented (no tabs) for a clean YAML literal block.

TDN ROUND-TRIP: all 13 params single-component (Int/Float) -> default->value seeding works on re-import (verified v6.0.26). Re-import the exported .tdn into a throwaway COMP, confirm a couple .vals + out1 renders, destroy it. operator_count counts all ops incl docked DATs (annotations not counted): ~12.
